title: Aterian, Inc. Q3 2023 Earnings Call Summary
text: Net revenue declined significantly to $39.7 million from $66.3 million year-over-year, representing a 40.2% decrease. The company attributed this primarily to reduced consumer discretionary spending and competitive pricing pressures on Amazon. Despite revenue decline, the company showed substantial progress toward profitability. The adjusted EBITDA loss improved 51% to $4.4 million from $9.1 million in Q3 2022. Net loss improved approximately 95% to $6.3 million compared to $116.9 million. Overall gross margin expanded to 49.4% from 45.5% year-over-year, driven by improved product mix and better pricing on liquidation sales. Management stated: We expect our go-forward business to consist of approximately 1,700 SKUs and approximately 50% reduction in our overall SKU count. The company discontinued underperforming products across all brands, with the most significant reductions in essential oils. The company is consolidating from 31 Amazon accounts down to 8 accounts, aiming for one account per brand to improve operational efficiency and reduce complexity. Recent launches include Squatty Potty products at Walmart and a TikTok Shop presence. Management remains confident in achieving adjusted EBITDA profitability by summer 2024, projecting Q4 2023 revenue between $28-32 million with an adjusted EBITDA loss of $6.5-7.5 million.
